source: 2026 EBE Undergraduate Handbook; 2026 General Rules and Policies Handbook

bas.name: Bachelor of Architectural Studies

curriculum_rules.label: Published BAS credit reconciliation
curriculum_rules.note: FB3.2 states a 432-credit minimum, while the printed BAS curriculum table totals fewer credits. Faculty confirmation is required before a graduation conclusion can be definitive.

progression_rules.label: No failed BAS major course
No course failed more than once
Pass at least 80% of annual registered credits
Complete within four years

award_notes: Awards remain subject to Senate approval under FB9.

geomatics_surveying_4.name: BSc Geomatics — Surveying (4-year)

curriculum_rules.label: Approved NQF level-7 elective
curriculum_rules.approval_note: The Surveying stream requires an approved 12-credit level-7 elective. APG3039B is recommended for an additional registration category, but other approvals require confirmation.

curriculum_rules.label: Cohort transition confirmation
curriculum_rules.note: The handbook states that a new 519-credit curriculum is being phased in from 2026 while a 576-credit curriculum is phased out. The student's intake-year curriculum must be confirmed.

progression_rules.label: First-year Geomatics progression
Rolling two-year credit rule
progression_rules.note: Subsequent progression uses a rolling two-year threshold (224 credits) and transfer/re-registration variants that require year-labelled records.

progression_notes: Fieldwork, practical performance and professional registration conditions remain operational requirements.
award_notes: Awards remain subject to Senate approval under FB9.

curriculum_rules.label: Science elective credits at NQF level 6 or above
Other approved Science-elective combination
curriculum_rules.note: The Programme Convener may approve other Science-elective combinations; a transcript alone cannot establish that approval.

curriculum_rules.label: Approved Humanities elective credits
curriculum_rules.approval_note: The EBE handbook lists these Humanities disciplines/courses, but offering, prerequisites and any exceptional approval still require confirmation.

curriculum_rules.label: Other approved advanced EBE elective
curriculum_rules.note: A third- or fourth-year EBE course outside the printed list may count only with Programme Convener approval and where content has not already been covered.

curriculum_rules.label: Advanced Engineering credits at NQF level 8
Free elective credits
curriculum_rules.approval_note: The handbook permits any UCT course for which prerequisites are met and content is not duplicated; the static catalogue cannot verify those conditions.

mechanical_engineering_4.name: BSc Engineering — Mechanical Engineering (4-year)

curriculum_rules.label: Approved complementary and open elective credits
curriculum_rules.approval_note: The route requires 15 approved category-(b) complementary-studies credits and 27 approved open-elective credits. The transcript does not establish category allocation, prerequisites, timetable fit or advisor approval.

curriculum_rules.label: Elective category allocation
curriculum_rules.note: Faculty confirmation is required that at least 15 credits satisfy complementary-studies category (b) and at least 27 credits satisfy the open-elective requirement.
